sections.heading: Past Tense Deep Dive
sections.body:
Regular past:
‚Ä¢ Most verbs: + ed (worked, played)
‚Ä¢ Ending in e: + d (lived, hoped)
‚Ä¢ Consonant + y: change to ied (studied, carried)
‚Ä¢ Short verbs ending in CVC: double consonant + ed (stopped, planned, cleaned)

Common irregular verbs (20 essential):
be‚Üíwas/were, have‚Üíhad, do‚Üídid, go‚Üíwent, see‚Üísaw,
say‚Üísaid, get‚Üígot, make‚Üímade, know‚Üíknew, think‚Üíthought,
come‚Üícame, take‚Üítook, give‚Üígave, find‚Üífound, tell‚Üítold,
ask‚Üíasked, work‚Üíworked, run‚Üíran, feel‚Üífelt, become‚Üíbecame

Past of "be":
‚Ä¢ I/he/she/it ‚Üí was
‚Ä¢ you/we/they ‚Üí were

sections.heading: Future Forms Comparison
sections.body:
1. Will ‚Äî predictions, promises, spontaneous decisions
"I will call you tomorrow."
"She will be 25 next year."

2. Going to ‚Äî plans and evidence-based predictions
"I'm going to start a business."
"Look! The car is going to hit the wall!"

3. Present continuous ‚Äî fixed arrangements
"We're meeting at 3pm tomorrow."
"I'm flying to London next week."

4. Present simple ‚Äî timetables and schedules
"The train leaves at 9am."
"School starts on Monday."

Decision guide:
‚Ä¢ Just decided? ‚Üí will
‚Ä¢ Planned in advance? ‚Üí going to
‚Ä¢ Already arranged with others? ‚Üí present continuous
‚Ä¢ Official schedule? ‚Üí present simple

sections.heading: Time Expressions
sections.body:
Past: yesterday, last week/month/year, ago, in 2020, when I was young
Present: now, today, this week, currently, at the moment
Future: tomorrow, next week/month/year, in 2030, soon, later

"Ago" vs "before":
‚Ä¢ "I saw him 3 days ago" (from now)
‚Ä¢ "I had seen him before" (before a past event)

"Will" vs "going to" in real examples:
"Why are you wearing a coat?" "It's going to rain" (I see dark clouds)
"Are you free tonight?" "Yes, I'll come" (just decided)
"I'm going to study medicine" (life plan, decided months ago)

summary: Past tense is straightforward once you memorize irregular verbs. Future has 4 forms ‚Äî pick based on how planned/spontaneous the action is.
